sections.label: Section 130
sections.original: φροῦδος πρὸς Αἴτνῃ θῆρας ἰχνεύων κυσίν .
sections.translation: He is gone hunting wild beasts with hounds on Aetna .

sections.label: Section 131
sections.original: οἶσθ ’ οὖν ὃ δρᾶσον , ὡς ἀπαίρωμεν χθονός ;
sections.translation: Dost know then what to do , that we may be gone from the land ?

sections.label: Section 132
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδ ’, Ὀδυσσεῦ · πᾶν δέ σοι δρῴημεν ἄν .
sections.translation: Not I , Odysseus ; but I would do anything for thee .

sections.label: Section 133
sections.original: ὅδησον ἡμῖν σῖτον , οὗ σπανίζομεν .
sections.translation: Sell us food , of which we are in need .

sections.label: Section 134
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν , ὥσπερ εἶπον , ἄλλο πλὴν κρέας .
sections.translation: There is nothing but flesh , as I said .

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἡδὺ λιμοῦ καὶ τόδε σχετήριον .
sections.translation: Well , even that is a pleasant preventive of hunger .

sections.label: Section 136
sections.original: καὶ τυρὸς ὀπίας ἔστι καὶ βοὸς γάλα .
sections.translation: And there is cheese curdled with fig-juice , and the milk of kine .

sections.label: Section 137
sections.original: ἐκφέρετε · φῶς γὰρ ἐμπολήμασιν πρέπει .
sections.translation: Bring them out ; a man should see his purchases .

sections.label: Section 138
sections.original: σὺ δ ’ ἀντιδώσεις , εἰπέ μοι , χρυσὸν πόσον ;
sections.translation: But tell me , how much gold wilt thou give me in exchange ?

sections.label: Section 139
sections.original: οὐ χρυσόν , ἀλλὰ πῶμα Διονύσου φέρω .
sections.translation: No gold bring I , but Dionysus ’ drink .

sections.label: Section 140
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατ ’ εἰπών , οὗ σπανίζομεν πάλαι .
sections.translation: Most welcome words ! I have long been wanting that .

sections.label: Section 141
sections.original: καὶ μὴν Μάρων μοι πῶμ ’ ἔδωκε , παῖς θεοῦ .
sections.translation: Yes , it was Maron , the god ’ s son , who gave me a draught .

sections.label: Section 142
sections.original: ὃν ἐξέθρεψα ταῖσδ ’ ἐγώ ποτ ’ ἀγκάλαις ;
sections.translation: What ! Maron whom once I dandled in these arms ?

sections.label: Section 143
sections.original: ὁ Βακχίου παῖς , ὡς σαφέστερον μάθῃς .
sections.translation: The son of the Bacchic god , that thou mayst learn more certainly .

sections.label: Section 144
sections.original: ἐν σέλμασιν νεώς ἐστιν , ἢ φέρεις σύ νιν ;
sections.translation: Is it inside the ship , or hast thou it with thee ?

sections.label: Section 145
sections.original: ὅδ ’ ἁσκὸς ὃς κεύθει νιν · ὡς ὁρᾷς , γέρον .
sections.translation: This , as thou seest , is the skin that holds it , old sir .

sections.label: Section 146
sections.original: οὗτος μὲν οὐδ ’ ἂν τὴν γνάθον πλήσειέ μου .
sections.translation: Why , that would not give me so much as a mouthful .

sections.label: Section 147
sections.original: ναί ·
sections.translation: This , and twice as much again as will run from the skin .

sections.label: Section 148
sections.original: καλήν γε κρήνην εἶπας ἡδεῖάν τ ’ ἐμοί .
sections.translation: Fair the rill thou speakest of , delicious to me .

sections.label: Section 149
sections.original: βούλῃ σε γεύσω πρῶτον ἄκρατον μέθυ ;
sections.translation: Shall I let thee taste the wine unmixed , to start with ?

sections.label: Section 150
sections.original: δίκαιον · ἦ γὰρ γεῦμα τὴν ὠνὴν καλεῖ .
sections.translation: A reasonable offer ; for of a truth a taste invites the purchase .

sections.label: Section 151
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐφέλκω καὶ ποτῆρ ’ ἀσκοῦ μέτα .
sections.translation: Well , I haul about a cup as well as the skin .

sections.label: Section 152
sections.original: φέρ ’ ἐκπάταξον , ὡς ἀναμνησθῶ πιών .
sections.translation: Come , let it gurgle in , that I may revive my memory by a pull at it .

sections.label: Section 153
sections.original: ἰδού .
sections.translation: There then !

sections.label: Section 153b
sections.original: παπαιάξ , ὡς καλὴν ὀσμὴν ἔχει .
sections.translation: Ye gods ! what a delicious scent it has !

sections.label: Section 154
sections.original: εἶδες γὰρ αὐτήν ;
sections.translation: What ! didst thou see it ?

sections.label: Section 154b
sections.original: οὐ μὰ Δί ’, ἀλλ ’ ὀσφραίνομαι .
sections.translation: No , i ’ faith , but I smell it .

sections.label: Section 155
sections.original: γεῦσαί νυν , ὡς ἂν μὴ λόγῳ ’’ παινῇς μόνον .
sections.translation: Taste it then , that thy approval may not stop at words .

sections.label: Section 156
sections.original: βαβαί · χορεῦσαι παρακαλεῖ μ ’ ὁ Βάκχιος .
sections.translation: Zounds ! Bacchus is inviting me to dance ; ha ! ha !

sections.label: Section 158
sections.original: μῶν τὸν λάρυγγα διεκάναξέ σου καλῶς ;
sections.translation: Did it not gurgle finely down thy throttle ?

sections.label: Section 159
sections.original: ὥστ ’ εἰς ἄκρους γε τοὺς ὄνυχας ἀφίκετο .
sections.translation: Aye that it did , to the ends of my fingers .

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: πρὸς τῷδε μέντοι καὶ νόμισμα δώσομεν .
sections.translation: Well , we will give thee money besides .

sections.label: Section 161
sections.original: χάλα τὸν ἀσκὸν μόνον · ἔα τὸ χρυσίον .
sections.translation: Only undo the skin , and never mind the money .

sections.label: Section 162
sections.original: ἐκφέρετέ νυν τυρεύματ ’ ἢ μήλων τόκον .
sections.translation: Bring out the cheeses then and lambs .

sections.label: Section 163
sections.original: δράσω τάδ ’, ὀλίγον φροντίσας γε δεσποτῶν .
sections.translation: I will do so , with small thought of any master . For let me have a single cup of that and I would turn madman ,

sections.label: Section 165
sections.original: πάντων Κυκλώπων ἀντιδοὺς βοσκήματα ,
sections.translation: giving in exchange for it the flocks of every Cyclops and then throwing myself into the sea from the Leucadian rock , once I have been well drunk and smoothed out my wrinkled brow . For if a man rejoice not in his drinking , he is mad ; for in drinking there is love

sections.label: Section 170
sections.original: μαστοῦ τε δραγμὸς καὶ παρεσκευασμένου
sections.translation: with all its frolic , and dancing withal , and oblivion of woe . Shall not I then purchase so rare a drink , bidding the senseless Cyclops and his central eye go hang ? Exit Silenus .

sections.label: Section 175
sections.original: ἄκου ’, Ὀδυσσεῦ · διαλαλήσωμέν τί σοι .
sections.translation: Hearken , Odysseus , let us hold some converse with thee .

sections.label: Section 176
sections.original: καὶ μὴν φίλοι γε προσφέρεσθε πρὸς φίλον .
sections.translation: Well , do so ; ours is a meeting of friends .

sections.label: Section 177
sections.original: ἐλάβετε Τροίαν τὴν Ἑλένην τε χειρίαν ;
sections.translation: Did you take Troy and capture the famous Helen ?

sections.label: Section 178
sections.original: καὶ πάντα γ ’ οἶκον Πριαμιδῶν ἐπέρσαμεν .
sections.translation: Aye , and we destroyed the whole family of Priam .

sections.label: Section 179
sections.original: οὔκουν , ἐπειδὴ τὴν νεᾶνιν εἵλετε ,
sections.translation: After capturing your blooming prize ,

sections.label: Section 180
sections.original: ἅπαντες αὐτὴν διεκροτήσατ ’ ἐν μέρει ,
sections.translation: were all of you in turn her lovers ? for she likes variety in husbands ; the traitress ! the sight of a man with embroidered breeches on his legs and a golden chain about his neck so fluttered her ,

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: ἐξεπτοήθη , Μενέλεων , ἀνθρώπιον
sections.translation: that she left Menelaus , her excellent little husband . Would there had never been a race of women born into the world at all , unless it were for me alone !

sections.label: Section 188
sections.original: ἰδοὺ τάδ ’ ὑμῖν ποιμένων βοσκήματα ,
sections.translation: ( reappearing with food .) Lo ! I bring you fat food from the flocks , king Odysseus , the young of bleating sheep

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: πηκτοῦ γάλακτός τ ’ οὐ σπάνια τυρεύματα .
sections.translation: and cheeses of curdled milk without stint . Carry them away with you and be gone from the cave at once , after giving me a drink of merry grape-juice in exchange .

sections.label: Section 193
sections.original: — οἴμοι · Κύκλωψ ὅδ ’ ἔρχεται · τί δράσομεν ;
sections.translation: Alack ! yonder comes the Cyclops ; what shall we do ?

sections.label: Section 194
sections.original: ἀπολώλαμεν γάρ , ὦ γέρον · ποῖ χρὴ φυγεῖν ;
sections.translation: Then truly are we lost , old sir ! whither must we fly ?

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: ἔσω πέτρας τῆσδ ’, οὗπερ ἂν λάθοιτέ γε .
sections.translation: Inside this rock , for there ye may conceal yourselves .

sections.label: Section 196
sections.original: δεινὸν τόδ ’ εἶπας , ἀρκύων μολεῖν ἔσω .
sections.translation: Dangerous advice of thine , to run into the net !

sections.label: Section 197
sections.original: οὐ δεινόν · εἰσὶ καταφυγαὶ πολλαὶ πέτρας .
sections.translation: No danger ; there are ways of escape in plenty in the rock .

sections.label: Section 198
sections.original: οὐ δῆτ ’· ἐπεί τἂν μεγάλα γ ’ ἡ Τροία στένοι ,
sections.translation: No , never that ; for surely Troy will groan and loudly too , if we flee from a single man , when I have oft withstood

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: Φρυγῶν ὑπέστην πολλάκις σὺν ἀσπίδι .
sections.translation: with my shield a countless host of Phrygians . Nay , if die we must , we will die a noble death ; or , if we live , we will maintain our old renown at least with credit .

sections.label: Section 203
sections.original: ἄνεχε · πάρεχε · τί τάδε · τίς ἡ ῥᾳθυμία ;
sections.translation: A light here ! hold it up ! what is this ? what means this idleness , your Bacchic revelry ? Here have we no Dionysus ,

sections.label: Section 205
sections.original: οὐ κρόταλα χαλκοῦ τυμπάνων τ ’ ἀράγματα .
sections.translation: nor clash of brass , nor roll of drums . Pray , how is it with my newly-born lambs in the caves ? are they at the teat , running close to the side of their dams ? Is the full amount of milk for cheeses milked out in baskets of rushes ?

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: τί φατε ; τί λέγετε ; τάχα τις ὑμῶν τῷ ξύλῳ
sections.translation: How now ? what say you ? One of ye will soon be shedding tears from the weight of my club ; look up , not down .

sections.label: Section 212
sections.original: ἰδού , πρὸς αὐτὸν τὸν Δί ’ ἀνακεκύφαμεν ,
sections.translation: There ! my head is bent back till I see Zeus himself ; I behold both the stars and Orion .

sections.label: Section 214
sections.original: ἄριστόν ἐστιν εὖ παρεσκευασμένον ;
sections.translation: Is my breakfast quite ready ?

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: πάρεστιν . ὁ φάρυγξ εὐτρεπὴς ἔστω μόνον .
sections.translation: ’ Tis laid ; be thy throat only ready .

sections.label: Section 216
sections.original: ἦ καὶ γάλακτός εἰσι κρατῆρες πλέῳ ;
sections.translation: Are the bowls too full of milk ?
